channel.title: Comments for page "Life in the Multiverse"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Life in the Multiverse"

item.title: Fixed Series I Tale List
item.content:encoded: Since the tale mentions 507, I added it below 507 on the series 1 tale list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Depends on the dialect.

From my experience it also appears to be grammatically correct, if obscure, to use the article according to how the words in the abbreviation are pronounced (as in, "a special containment procedure" = "a SCP").

Not that I care; I probably used 10 grammatical errors in this comment. But I'm just sayin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how could an entire continent be a SCP?

Should be "an" instead of "a", because when annunciating the letter "S" phonetically, a vowel sound is used (ess).

EDIT: Went and fixed it myself after reviewing what I am and am not allowed to edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: amen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Multiverse stuff is some of my favorite stuff on the site. Especially the way it's presented here. If I had any complaints, it's that it all seems to be (for the most part) similar and congruent (I think that's the right usage of the word) to our reality, which is probably a necessary flaw to keep interest in the story and write it. I do dig the two multiverses with no gravity and where Earth is a gas giant, however. I wish those were explored more.

Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a sucker for this kind of thing when done well, and… this is done well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really loved this.

That being said, I did not really, really love the ending. It seemed a little weak to me.

Nevertheless, I enjoyed this enough to upvote without any second thoughts. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
not the sound, the letter. The curvy glyph, combined with the sound, combined with the knowledge that in this context it represents the word "Secure", combined with the meaning of the word "Secure" in this context.

Memetic kill agents are more complex than you think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"…A-2190-Omega could go fuck itself.

F-2190-Omega… "

I missed the diferenciation for A and F, or is it a mistake? honestly not sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the Republic of Australia-Hungary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By the way. No one even realized that he put SCP-1438 instead of 1483?

I ficed it. emmm, sorry for the inconveneince

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the image of the green-skinned guy trying to understand something that basically (and literally) exploded within his heas.

Wait a second. What if the sound "S" is a memetic kill agent in that universe? a evry nasty one. God, I think I'm gonna upvote this again…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very Sliders-ish, which I quite liked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried to upvote this twice. Delightfully silly and imaginative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really like this. Especially the universe where heads explode when you say "S" and the saw-toothed-fish the size of blimps-universe. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool concept, but the prose and dialogue grated me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted halfway through got to the bottom and tried to upvote again. This is some really good writing. Quirky but not too much so. Really liked this lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a little on the zany side, but I like it. If you crossed the Foundation with Stargate, I have to imagine it would look something like this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bit of author commentary here:

Tom, Trevor and Tristan have all been seen in Djoric's tales Multi-U 101, and Djoric gave me permission to use these characters. Tom is responsible for initial expeditions into new universes, usually Floaters (ones that can't support life as we know it), and loves his job greatly. Trevor is a diplmoat, negotiating trade and peace agreements between universes and entities within them. Tristan was a researcher, who researched the culture and history of various universes before his brothers got transferred, at which point he took over Trevor's job.

This fits into the S & C Plastics mini-canon, as all three of them worked at Site 87 before being transferred.

This takes place before any other S & C Plastics tales written so far.
